name: Key West
summary: Key West is generally safe for tourists with low violent crime but higher than average petty crime rates. The island's laid-back atmosphere and tourism infrastructure provide good safety conditions, though visitors should remain vigilant during nightlife activities.
quickVerdict: Key West is a safe destination for most travelers, with violent crime being relatively rare and the island maintaining a welcoming, tourist-friendly environment. While petty crime like theft occurs more frequently in touristy areas, proper precautions and common sense will ensure a safe visit. The main concerns are hurricane risk during summer months and staying alert during the vibrant nightlife scene.

neighborhoods.name: Old Town/Duval Street
neighborhoods.description: The main tourist district with heavy foot traffic, well-lit streets, and regular police presence. Generally very safe but watch for pickpockets in crowds.

neighborhoods.name: Mallory Square
neighborhoods.description: Popular sunset viewing area with constant crowds and good lighting. Very safe during events but keep valuables secure.

neighborhoods.name: Truman Annex
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential area with luxury accommodations and excellent security. One of the safest parts of the island.

neighborhoods.name: Stock Island
neighborhoods.description: Lower income area with higher crime rates. Visitors should exercise extra caution, especially at night.

neighborhoods.name: Bahama Village
neighborhoods.description: Historic neighborhood undergoing gentrification. Generally safe during the day but use caution after dark.

neighborhoods.name: Casa Marina Area
neighborhoods.description: Resort area near the airport with good security and lighting. Safe for tourists but less nightlife activity.

scams.name: Vacation Rental Fraud
scams.description: Fake rental listings on Craigslist and other sites using stolen photos of legitimate properties. Victims arrive to find no reservation.
scams.howToAvoid: Only book through verified rental companies with valid Transient Licenses. If the price seems too good to be true, it probably is. Verify the property address and company credentials.

scams.name: Duval Street Shopping Scams
scams.description: T-shirt shops and souvenir stores use bait-and-switch pricing, advertising low prices but adding excessive charges for custom work.
scams.howToAvoid: Get written price quotes before agreeing to any custom work. Avoid high-pressure sales tactics and shops with aggressive street hawkers.

scams.name: Fake Tour Operators
scams.description: Unlicensed individuals approach tourists offering discounted boat trips or excursions that don't exist or are substandard.
scams.howToAvoid: Book tours only through established companies with physical locations and good online reviews. Avoid street solicitors offering deals that seem too good to be true.

scams.name: Overpriced Fine Jewelry
scams.description: Jewelry stores targeting tourists with dubious quality diamonds and gems at inflated prices using high-pressure sales tactics.
scams.howToAvoid: Avoid duty-free jewelry stores and high-pressure sales environments. Purchase jewelry from reputable local jewelers you've researched beforehand.

soloFemale.overview: Key West is considered very safe for solo female travelers with its welcoming, inclusive atmosphere and strong tourism infrastructure. The island has a laid-back vibe and locals are generally friendly and helpful. Most areas are well-lit and heavily trafficked.
tips: Stay in well-lit, populated areas especially on Duval Street and Old Town
Don't overindulge in alcohol and always keep drinks in sight
Use ride-sharing services or taxis rather than walking alone late at night
Book accommodations in safer neighborhoods like Truman Annex or Old Town
Trust your instincts and avoid any situation that feels uncomfortable

nightSafety.overview: Key West's nightlife is legendary but generally safe when proper precautions are taken. Duval Street and surrounding areas have good lighting and police presence. The main risks involve alcohol-related incidents and petty crime targeting intoxicated tourists.
tips: Stick to busy, well-lit areas like Duval Street and avoid dark alleys
Travel in groups when possible and don't wander off alone
Pace your drinking and stay aware of your surroundings
Use reputable transportation to get back to your accommodation
Keep valuables secure and don't flash large amounts of cash

transport.metro: No metro system exists. Key West has a free Duval Loop shuttle that runs through Old Town and along Duval Street, stopping at major attractions.
transport.rideshare: Uber and Lyft operate throughout the island with good availability. Generally safe and reliable for getting around, especially at night.
transport.taxis: Traditional taxis are available and safe, though more expensive than rideshare options. Pedicabs offer a fun alternative for short distances.
transport.tips: Biking is very popular and safe with required lights at night. Walking is feasible for most attractions in Old Town. Avoid renting a car unless planning trips outside Key West.

customs: Tipping is expected at restaurants, bars, and for services - typically 18-20%
Key West has a very relaxed dress code; casual beach attire is acceptable almost everywhere
The island is extremely LGBTQ+ friendly with an inclusive and welcoming atmosphere
Alcohol is prohibited on public beaches and streets, including Duval Street
Locals are called Conchs - don't feed the wild roosters and chickens as it's illegal

health.overview: Key West has adequate medical facilities for basic needs with emergency services available. More serious conditions may require airlift to Miami. The main health risks are sun exposure and dehydration.
health.water: Tap water is generally safe but comes from mainland Florida. Some visitors prefer bottled water. Water quality can be affected by storms or infrastructure issues.
health.vaccinations: No special vaccinations required beyond standard US recommendations. Mosquito protection recommended for comfort and to prevent mosquito-borne illnesses.
health.altitude: Sea level location with subtropical climate. Extreme heat and humidity in summer require frequent hydration and sun protection.

faq.q: Is Key West safe for tourists?
faq.a: Yes, Key West is generally very safe for tourists. Violent crime is rare, and the island caters heavily to tourism with good infrastructure and security. Use normal travel precautions and you should have no issues.

faq.q: Is Key West safe at night?
faq.a: Key West is relatively safe at night, especially in the main tourist areas like Duval Street which are well-lit and patrolled. Stay in groups when possible, don't overdrink, and use rideshare services to get home safely.

faq.q: Is Key West safe for solo female travelers?
faq.a: Key West is considered very safe for solo female travelers. The island has an inclusive, welcoming atmosphere. Stay aware of your surroundings, don't overindulge in alcohol, and stick to well-lit, populated areas.

faq.q: What areas should I avoid in Key West?
faq.a: Stock Island and parts of Bahama Village have higher crime rates and should be avoided, especially at night. Stick to Old Town, Duval Street, and established resort areas for the safest experience.

faq.q: Is it safe to use public transport in Key West?
faq.a: Yes, the free Duval Loop shuttle is safe and convenient. Biking is very popular and safe with proper lights. Uber, Lyft, and taxis are all reliable options for getting around the island.
